If you have received this telecopy in error, please immediately notify us by telephone at the number above to arrange for return of the original document to us. Thank you. Patient Name: BIZIMANA, OLIVIER, DOB: 08/12/1982, Account No: 34131 REFERRAL Jina H Naaim, PA-C OLIVIER BIZIMANA Pain Medicine 08/12/1982 Central Phoenix Innovative Pain and Wellness 1277 E MISSOURI AVE STE 102, PHOENIX, AZ-85014-2916 Tel: 480-467-2273 Fax: 602-547-6887 Date: 03/30/2026 Patient Information: Patient Name: OLIVIER BIZIMANA Patient DOB: 08/12/1982 Patient Insurance: Medicare Part B Patient Subscriber No: 1Y63RT9JR08 Patient Address: 8330 N 19TH AVE, APT 2056, PHOENIX, AZ 85021-5287 US Patient Phone: 623-227-6352 Patient Work Phone: Patient Cell Phone: 623-227-6352 Patient SSN: XXX-XX-1686 Insurance Information Insurance Name: Medicare Part B Subscriber Name: BIZIMANA, OLIVIER Subscriber DOB: 08/12/1982 Subscriber No: 1Y63RT9JR08 Subscriber Group No: Subscriber Address: 8330 N 19TH AVE, APT 2056, PHOENIX, AZ 85021-5287 US Subscriber Phone: 623-227-6352 Referral From Information: Provider Name: Naaim, Jina, H Provider ID Number: Provider UPIN: Provider NPI: 1952128381 Provider Facility: Central Phoenix - Innovative Pain and Wellness Provider Speciality: Pain Medicine Address1: 1277 E MISSOURI AVE Address2: STE 102 City, State, Zip: PHOENIX, AZ, 85014-2916 Phone: 480-467-2273 Fax: 602-547-6887 Referral To Information: Provider Name: Provider ID Number: Provider UPIN: Provider NPI: Provider Facility: Provider Speciality: Address1: Address2: City, State, Zip: Phone: Appt. Date/Time: Fax: Facility Tax ID Number: REFERRAL Jina H Naaim, PA-C OLIVIER BIZIMANA Pain Medicine 08/12/1982 Central Phoenix - Innovative Pain and Wellness 1277 E MISSOURI AVE STE 102, PHOENIX, AZ-85014-2916 Tel: 480-467-2273 Fax: 602-547-6887 To: STAR, Subject: Referrals, Fax#: 4809560654, SendDate: 03/30/2026 12:43:41 AM, page 1/10 [-ufg2.14.5in] Patient Name: BIZIMANA, OLIVIER, DOB: 08/12/1982, Account No: 34131 Reason For Referral: Authorization No: Authorization Type: Reason: AT HOME STAR PHYSICAL THERAPY- eval and tx for lumbar radiculopathy. Diagnosis: M54.16 - Lumbar radiculopathy E/M Codes: Procedures: Visits Allowed: 99 Unit Type: V (VISIT) Start Date: 03/30/2026 End Date: 03/30/2027 Priority: Routine Provider NPI: 1952128381 Electronically signed by Jina H Naaim, PA-C on 03/30/2026 at 12:43 PM MDT To: STAR, Subject: Referrals, Fax#: 4809560654, SendDate: 03/30/2026 12:43:41 AM, page 2/10 [-ufg2.14.5in] Patient Name: BIZIMANA, OLIVIER, DOB: 08/12/1982, Account No: 34131 Progress Note Progress Notes Patient: BIZIMANA, OLIVIER Provider: Jina H Naaim, PA-C Account Number: 34131 DOB: 08/12/1982 Age: 43 Y Sex: Male Date: 03/30/2026 Phone: 623-227-6352 Address: 8330 N 19TH AVE, APT 2056, PHOENIX, AZ-85021-5287 Subjective: Chief Complaints: 1. MRI REVIEW. HPI: 1. History of Problem:: Reason for today's visit? Back pain. Location of Pain? Lumber, thighs, legs and feet. Does you pain radiate? If so where? The pain radiates from the lumbar spine to the feet. What is your current pain on a scale of 0-10? 8. What is your average pain on a scale of 0-10? 6. Has your pain changed since last office visit? No. What is the frequency of your pain? Constant. Describe your pain: Aching,Burning,Tingling,Numbness. What makes your pain worse? Standing, Sitting, Walking, Twisting, Exercises. What makes your pain better? Laying down, medications. 2. Medication History:: How much pain relief/functional improvement does your current medication provide? 10-30%. Do you feel that you can use less medication than currently prescribed? No. Are you having any side effects from your medication? Yes. When was the last time you took pain medication? I have been out of medication. 3. Treatment History:: Are you currently taking Anticoagulants/ Blood Thinners? No. What doctor manages the blood thinner? None. Why are you taking a blood thinner? None. Have you had any procedures to treat your pain in the last two months? No. If yes, how much pain relief did you have? NA. Did you have any complications from the procedure? No. Are you currently attending conservative treatment including PT/Chiro/Massage/Acupuncture? If so which one? No. How many sessions have you completed? NA. Percentage of relief obtained from conservative treatment? 0%. 4. Diagnostic Studies: Have you completed any Imaging or Diagnostic studies since last office visit? No. ROS: General / Constitutional: Change in appetite denies. Chills denies. Weight gain denies. Weight loss denies. Weakness denies. Respiratory: Shortness of breath denies. Cardiovascular: Chest pain denies. Palpitations denies. Gastrointestinal: Constipation denies. Diarrhea denies. Nausea denies. Vomiting denies. Hematology: Bleeding problems denies. Neurologic: Dizziness denies. Fainting denies. Gait abnormality denies. Medical History: No medical problems. Surgical History: lumbar discectomy at L5 8/17/2022, back surgery 05/2025, Lumbar laminectomy LEFT L4 and L5 05/2025. Hospitalization/Major Diagnostic Procedure: fall 2015, denies recent 03/2023. Family History: no family hx. Screening: Social History: Migrated Social History: Drugs/Alcohol: (Alcohol Screen): No Did you have a drink containing alcohol in the past year?: No, Points: 0, Interpretation: Negative. Tobacco Use: (Tobacco Use/Smoking) No Are you a: : nonsmoker. Medications: Taking Gabapentin 300 MG Capsule 1 capsule Orally three times a day, Notes to Pharmacist: Do not dispense until 30 days after last fill, or on the last business day prior to 30 days in cases where pharmacy is closed on To: STAR, Subject: Referrals, Fax#: 4809560654, SendDate: 03/30/2026 12:43:41 AM, page 3/10 [-ufg2.14.5in] Patient Name: BIZIMANA, OLIVIER, DOB: 08/12/1982, Account No: 34131 30th day. Don't drink alcohol while taking this medication. Objective: Vitals: Wt: 230 lbs, Ht: 72 in, BP: 130/76 mm Hg, HR: 60, Oxygen sat %: 96 %, Pain scale: 8 1-10, Wt-kg: 104.33, BMI: 31.19, Body Surface Area: 2.3. Examination: General Examination: General: Awake, alert, and oriented HEENT: atraumatic, mucous membranes moist Respiratory: Normal respiratory excursion Cardiovascular: Well perfused, no jvd Extremities: No clubbing, Cyanosis or edema Skin: Warm, dry, intact. no rashes on exposed skin. MSK: Chronic arthritic changes in multiple joints Back Exam: Inspection: no muscle atrophy, reversal of normal lordosis and exaggeration of thoracic kyphosis Palpation: TTP bilateral facet joints and paraspinal musculature ROM: limited especially lateral rotation, pain on flexion and lateral rotation Facet loading: RIGHT- positive/ LEFT- positive. Assessment: Assessment: 1. Lumbar radiculopathy - M54.16 (Primary) 2. Spondylosis of lumbosacral joint without myelopathy - M47.817 3. Neuropathy - G62.9 4. Long term (current) use of opiate analgesic - Z79.891 History- He incurred pain on August 15, 2015, he slid and fell on the floor when he was mopping. He began to feel numbness and weakness to his legs. He saw pain management and had two epidural injections. This did not help. He did physical therapy in 2015 and repeated again this year, the month prior to this initial appointment. He had discectomy on 8/17/22 at the back institute in Los Angeles. He was searching online and found this surgeon and had great testimonies. Per Dr. Jagadesh, no surgery indicated. He has more pain now. He cannot stand for longer than 10 minutes whereas before he could for 30 minutes. He is not able to work. I reviewed CT of the lumbar spine performed on August 16, 2022 which revealed mild posterior disc bulge at multiple levels. There is mild facet arthropathy from L1-L5. There is moderate canal stenosis at L5-S1 with mild to moderate bilateral neural foraminal narrowing. At L4-5 there is severe central canal stenosis and moderate bilateral neural foraminal narrowing. L3-4 there is severe central canal stenosis as well as at L2-3. Reviewed an x-ray of the lumbar spine which revealed no evidence of fracture. EMG that was performed in 9/30/2022 was normal per patient. It was recommended that he have a neurological consultation since he has not had relief from spinal injections and was not a surgical candidate. There may be a neurological condition that may be causing his pain. Pt did have neurology follow up and had a new LE EMG. Per report he is positive for L L3-4 radiculopathy. This was not identified on his last EMG. Today: patient is a pleasant 43-year-old male who is presenting for lumbar radiculopathy down the back of both legs. He reports that he did have a left L4 and L5 laminectomy in Africa about a year ago. Reports that he continues to have pain down both legs. Discussed lumbar MRI in detail today with patient. He would like to proceed with an epidural. He reports no surgeries at L5 and S1. Discussed ILESI L5/S1 in detail with patient. He would like to proceed. Denies any use of blood thinners. Reports that he would like to trial physical therapy. He would also like to hold off any surgical evaluations. Denies neurological deficits, red flag symptoms, or severe pain. ER precautions given. He would like a Toradol injection today due to pain. He reports that pain is worsened with standing for long period of time, walking for long period of time, sitting to standing positions as well. No pain in the buttocks or SI joint. Reports that he cannot do much for over 30 minutes before having pain. Discussed conservative measures and activity modification in detail as well. He would like to have increase in gabapentin. he would like to trial 400 mg 3 times daily first before increasing any further. Declines LSO today. To: STAR, Subject: Referrals, Fax#: 4809560654, SendDate: 03/30/2026 12:43:41 AM, page 4/10 [-ufg2.14.5in] Patient Name: BIZIMANA, OLIVIER, DOB: 08/12/1982, Account No: 34131 The patient presents with chronic *BACK* pain radiating into the **lower** extremity in a dermatomal distribution consistent with [L5/S1] radiculopathy. Pain has been present for approximately > 6 weeks. Physical examination demonstrates objective evidence of nerve root involvement, including + straight leg raise reproducing radicular symptoms, decreased sensation in the corresponding dermatome MRI of the lumbar spine dated shows stenosis at L4-5 and L5-S1 with impingement of the nerve roots, correlating with the patient's symptoms and exam findings. The patient has participated in and remains engaged in conservative management, including a structured physical therapy program from (core stabilization, stretching, and functional restoration) and an ongoing home exercise program performed at least 2 times per week for 6 weeks as well as trials of medications such as NSAIDs, neuropathic agents, activity modification, and use of heat/ice, without adequate or durable relief. Despite this conservative care over more than [6] weeks, the patient continues to experience function-limiting radicular pain that interferes with [walking/standing/sitting/sleep/ADLs)] An epidural steroid injection is requested as a medically necessary next step to reduce nerve root inflammation, improve pain, and facilitate further participation in active rehabilitation. The procedure will be performed under continuous fluoroscopic guidance with non-ionic contrast dye to confirm correct epidural placement and spread prior to steroid injection, in accordance with safety and Medicare requirements. Diagnostic Testing: -Lumbar MRI 03/2026 on chart and reviewed with patient in detail. MRI L/S 4/18/24 attached in pt's chart MRI L/S 10/4/22: Modic changes noted, full report attached in pt chart. MRI L/S 10/12/22 (rad impression): L5-S1 broad-based disc protrusion 4 mm with annular visual tear epidural space. Right neural foraminal narrowing. Grade 1 retrolisthesis of L5 over S1. Full report attached to pt chart, not compaired to prior imaging. Bilateral lower extremity EMG 9/30/2022: This is a essentially normal study providing no convincing electrophysiological evidence of large fiber peripheral neuropathy, right cervical/lumbar sacral radiculopathy or upper/lower extremity mono neuropathy to explain patient's symptoms. Medications: restart Gabapentin 300mg TID - increase 400mg TID DC pregabalin 100mg caused loss of vision DC Duloxetine 30mg qAM, ineffective DC nabumetone, ineffective MDP completed Oct 2022 - no relief of pain RF Tramadol 50 mg BID OA 8/2024 UDS C/S 08/2024 Neg Interventional Procedures: :LEFT L4/5 LAMI PER PT IN AFRICA - Recommend ILESI at L5-S1 8/26/24 B/L L3-5 MBB he reports no relief 10/16/2023 L3/4 ILESI no relief Conservative Therapies: Completed PT Sept 2022 Consider repeat FCT DME Referrals/Specialists: - Cont with neurologist - Referred to Dr. Valdes - recommended surgery; pt declined - Continue with psych for MDD and Gen Anxiety - Referral to new spine surgeon 10/31/24 declines new referral to spine surgeon follow up 4 weeks. The patient's initial evaluation was performed by a physician. Today's visit continues and is consistent with the patient's previously established plan of care and is billed incident to, as there is a doctor who is immediately available in person or via tele-communication. The supervising physician, Dr. Haddad, has reviewed the plan of care and is in agreement, including with the medications prescribed, (if applicable) for this patient. To: STAR, Subject: Referrals, Fax#: 4809560654, SendDate: 03/30/2026 12:43:41 AM, page 5/10 [-ufg2.14.5in] Patient Name: BIZIMANA, OLIVIER, DOB: 08/12/1982, Account No: 34131 TTIIS note was transchbed using electronic sonware and voice recognition sonware. TIMS note may contain innerent spelling, grammatical or dictation inaccuracies due to software. Although proofreading was performed, some errors do persist. Please feel free to contact our office with any clarification or questions. OPIATE PAIN MEDICATIONS: The risks of opiate pain medications have been discussed with the patient. These risks include addiction, medication tolerance, physical dependence, respiratory depression, constipation, nausea, an opioid- induced hyperalgesic state (increase in pain from opioid medications) and even death. Tolerance can limit the efficacy of opioids for long-term pain management. The patient has been warned to keep their medication in a safe place, completely secured from unauthorized access by children or others in the home. The best way to store these medications is in a lockable container. While taking opiate medications patients are instructed to abstain from using alcohol. While taking pain medications, patients are cautioned about driving or operating heavy machinery while taking these medications due to the risk of impairment and motor vehicle accident. They are never to drive if they are impaired in any way. The patient has signed an opioid treatment agreement and states they are taking the medication as prescribed. Pharmacy board report reviewed today and consistent with history. At present the patient reports that the medication significantly improves their pain/quality of life and provides functional improvement. The patient provided their informed consent to continue opiate therapy as they believe that the benefits outweigh these risks. As this patient is clearly uncomfortable today, the patient has been provided with a Toradol injection, 30 mg IM to afford some palliation of the acute pain. The patient tolerated the injection well and was discharged in a stable condition. Vital Signs Stable upon discharge. Plan: Treatment: 1. Lumbar radiculopathy Refill Gabapentin Capsule, 400 MG, 1 capsule, Orally, three times a day, 30 days, 90 Capsule, Refills 0, Notes to Pharmacist: Do not dispense until 30 days after last fill, or on the last business day prior to 30 days in cases where pharmacy is closed on 30th day. Don't drink alcohol while taking this medication. Procedure: Lumbar IL ESI ILESI L5/S1 WITH IVCS NO CC Procedure: Toradol IM 30 mg IM Referral To: Reason:AT HOME STAR PHYSICAL THERAPY- eval and tx for lumbar radiculopathy. Procedure Codes: J1885 INJ KETOROLAC TROMETHAMINE 15 MG Follow Up: after esi. Billing Information: Visit Code: 99214 Office Visit, Est Pt., Level 4.
